sequences.title: Helm sequence delivery refactor (8-phase)
sequences.missionStatement: Unify Helm/MCP sequence delivery through shared executor, refactor helm-control-service into focused modules, add tests, update docs
sequences.sharedMemory:
## Shared Context

**Problem:** Helm/MCP delivery bypasses the sequence executor, so `{Send}`, `{NoSend}`, `{Wait}` tokens don't work in Telegram → Helm → CLI. Additionally, helm-control-service.ts is monolithic (~1000 lines) mixing 9 responsibilities.

**Solution:** Route all command delivery through `executeSequenceString`, extract `src/session/sequence-delivery.ts` helper, refactor service into 8 focused modules.

## Phases Overview

- **Phases 1-5 (Plan A):** Extract helpers, patch delivery paths, search & classify
- **Phases 6-7 (Plan B):** Refactor giant service, add tests
- **Phase 8 (Plan C):** Update MCP guide documentation

## Key Files

- `src/mcp/helm-control-service.ts` (~1000 lines) — monolith to refactor
- `src/input/sequence-executor.ts` — existing shared executor
- `src/session/sequence-delivery.ts` — new helper to create
- `src/mcp/submit-suffix.ts` — new, extracted from service

## Acceptance Criteria (All Plans)

1. No direct `ptyManager.deliverText(..., { submitSuffix })` for command text
2. `{Send}`, `{NoSend}`, `{Wait}` work in `session_send_text.text`
3. Recipient CLI submit suffix is correctly applied
4. Service refactored into domain-focused modules
5. Tests cover suffix parsing and sequence delivery
6. Guide docs explain token support
7. No breaking changes to MCP contracts
